evidence_quotes.quote: software and video games included in the digital fairness act.
With the rules already proposed to protect buyers, this can also apply to live service games.
Games that are no longer available but have only been preserved by initiatives by people holding these games.
a long document that almost no customer can read

paragraphs.text: I would like to see software and video games included in the digital fairness act. Many of the “terms of service” rules of many companies allow them, without pardon, to buy software from you where you pay. In the case of my personal experience, there are such rules that make the digital service and alsog the publisher of video games say it is a licence. While there are also services that sell video games digitally and you have the right to keep them even if the service goes offline. I bought several games digitally to keep space. But now I am aware that I can simply be repackaged that is mentioned in a long document that almost no customer can read or that it does not actually spend money on a product that you cannot retain under their conditions. So I would like to ask you, legislators and politicians in the EU to be video games and software that only buy digitally if something that then becomes the property of the buyer as it is with a physical copy of a video game or software placed on a CD. That people do not spend their money in something that can easily be taken away while people thought it would be cheaper digitally. The fact that it is equally owned but appears to be somewhat time-consuming. It is a problem if there are no physical copies and that it can only be purchased digitally. I have this experience with several games that I hold digitally but cannot obtain a physical copy of them. Games that are no longer available but have only been preserved by initiatives by people holding these games. With the rules already proposed to protect buyers, this can also apply to live service games. These are games that are capable of exerting social pressure on people who buy nothing there to continue playing or to show their avatar mooier. It would be beautiful that these live service games could continue to be played by their fans. So far, this is not possible and, as a result, players lose everything they have invested in such a live service game. I would like people to continue playing these live service games and keep their digital objects in some way. In any event, I consider it unacceptable that everything that has been purchased digitally can simply disappear, that should not be. Like a physical object, people should be able to reinstall it on a new game computer, PC or laptop. So again, I would like to draw your attention to the examination of software and video games.
